sourceMovie.title: Michael Clayton
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Michael Clayton for the same reason it broke through in 2007: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Gilroy keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Michael Clayton clicked for you, Donnie Brasco (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Michael Clayton?
faq.answer: Try Donnie Brasco, The Bone Collector, Side Effects—each shares DNA with Michael Clayton while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Michael Clayton?
faq.answer: Michael Clayton (2007) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Michael Clayton worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
